entities.name: SIX-STEP STRATEGY FOR BREAKING BAD NEWS
entities.description: A structured approach involving key communication techniques to facilitate information flow during bad news interviews.

entities.name: four essential goals
entities.description: Objectives in the process of disclosing bad news: gathering information, providing intelligible information, supporting the patient, and developing a strategy (treatment plan).

entities.name: gathering information from the patient
entities.description: Determining the patient's knowledge, expectations, and readiness to hear unfavorable information.

entities.name: providing intelligible information in accordance with the patient’ s needs and desires
entities.description: Communicating unfavorable information clearly and appropriately based on the patient's individual requirements.

entities.name: support the patient by employing skills to reduce the emotional impact and isolation experienced by the recipient of bad news
entities.description: Using communication techniques to lessen the negative emotional effects and feelings of loneliness associated with receiving difficult news.

entities.name: develop a strategy in the form of a treatment plan
entities.description: Creating a course of action, often including medical treatments, following the disclosure of unfavorable information.

entities.name: Prostate-specific membrane antigen
entities.description: A protein highly and specifically expressed on the surface of prostate tumor cells at all tumor stages. It is located in the cytosol in normal prostate cells but becomes membrane-bound in prostatic carcinoma. Its expression rises with increasing tumor dedifferentiation and in metastatic/hormone-refractory cancers. Also found in neovasculature of tumors and lower levels in brain, kidneys, salivary glands, small intestine.

entities.name: acquisition times
entities.description: Can be reduced by using LAFOV (Large Axial Field of View) scanners compared to SAFOV (Small Axial Field of View) systems, especially for later imaging points like 4h.

entities.name: SAFOV scanner
entities.description: Standard acquisition time is recommended as 2 min/bp. For a previous generation SAFOV system in continuous bed motion, this could take up to 16 min.

entities.name: LAFOV scanner
entities.description: Able to capture a 106 cm FOV in a single acquisition. A 16 min/bp acquisition (as performed at 4h) would be impracticably long with SAFOV systems.

entities.name: radiopharmaceutical activity
entities.description: Decreases over time due to decay and biological half-life. After 4 hours, less than 17 MBq of an initially applied 192 MBq would be available.

entities.name: image quality
entities.description: Can be detrimental using SAFOV systems with later acquisitions, but not necessarily for LAFOV systems. Achieving high-quality image possible even after nearly four half-lives.

entities.name: 2.5D Convolutional Neural Network

entities.name: 2.5D CNN
entities.description: Takes three neighboring slices as input to predict the result of the middle slice, enabling learning of third-dimension information between slices.

entities.name: triple-combining 2.5D U-Net
entities.description: Enhances the ability to learn 3D context information by extracting salient features from PET and CT scans simultaneously in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes.

entities.name: information-extraction component
entities.description: Includes three 2.5D U-Net networks designed for extracting features from three different planes (axial, coronal, sagittal).

entities.name: fusion component
entities.description: Synthesizes information extracted by the information-extraction component to predict the final result using a majority voting strategy.

entities.name: U-Net
entities.description: Employed in the information extraction component; comprises a down-sampling path (encoder stacks) and an up-sampling path (decoder stacks), using skip-connections for localization.

entities.name: down-sampling path
entities.description: Part of the U-Net architecture; aggregates increasingly abstract information (encoder stacks).

entities.name: up-sampling path
entities.description: Part of the U-Net architecture; recombines aggregated information with shallower features for precise localization (decoder stacks).

entities.name: skip-connection
entities.description: Used in the U-Net architecture, specifically concatenation.
